title: America's New AI Power
text:
Uncle Sam has become the gatekeeper to frontier AI-a new source of extraordinary leverage

THE NEWS is full of how an ignominious peace deal with Iran exemplifies a decline in American power (see Leader).

That conclusion could hardly be more wrong. On June 12th the Trump administration ordered Anthropic to block foreigners from Fable and Mythos, its latest and most capable frontier AI models. In an instant, everyone learned that the American gov ernment can decide who may use the world's most important technology. You don't get much more powerful than that.

The administration was responding to a supposed jailbreak for Fable, meaning a prompt that circumvents defences against uses such as hacking computers or making biowea-pons. The chances are that it wanted Anthropic to switch off the models for everyone, and that targeting foreigners was a means to an end. Sure enough, that is what Anthropic did, while claiming that the concern about its model was over-blown. The legal basis of the order remains unclear, and the ban seems unlikely to last.

What matters, though, is the demonstration that global ac-cess to the best Al may come down to a decision in the Oval Office. The administration showed in March that it is prepared to trample on the frontier Al companies, when it designated Anthropic a "supply-chain risk". Now it has shown that it is prepared to trample on users, too.

quizlet: a decision in the Oval Office
latest and most capable frontier AI models

questions.question: Why does the author argue that claims about America's declining power are mistaken?
options: Because America has reduced military spending
Because the US government can control access to frontier AI
Because Europe depends on American energy
Because China has stopped developing AI
questions.explanation: The article argues that America's ability to decide who can use the world's leading AI models demonstrates extraordinary geopolitical power.

questions.question: Why did the Trump administration order Anthropic to block foreign users?
options: To reduce server costs
Because of concerns about a possible jailbreak
To prepare for a stock-market listing
To increase subscription prices
questions.explanation: The administration responded to reports of a possible jailbreak that could bypass the model's safety protections.

questions.question: What does the article suggest about the legal basis of the government's order?
options: It is clearly established
It was approved by Congress
It remains uncertain
It was supported by the United Nations
questions.explanation: The author states that the legal basis of the order remains unclear.

questions.question: According to the article, what is the most significant consequence of the Anthropic incident?
options: AI companies will move abroad
The AI models became permanently unavailable
The world learned that access to frontier AI may depend on the US government
Anthropic stopped developing new models
questions.explanation: The key issue is not the temporary ban itself but the demonstration that Washington can control global access to frontier AI.

questions.question: What criticism does the author make of the US administration?
options: It ignored AI safety concerns
It was willing to override both AI companies and their users
It invested too little in AI research
It refused to regulate AI companies
questions.explanation: The article argues that the administration first pressured frontier AI companies and then showed it was also prepared to restrict users.
